title: Daily Source Digest
note: Daily source-balanced news digest — morning update.

stories.title: Prosecutors won’t seek death penalty in Minnesota Democrat’s assassination
stories.summary:
Prosecutors won’t seek death penalty in Minnesota Democrat’s assassination. U.S. prosecutors announced Wednesday that they would not seek the death penalty for the man charged with the political assassination of a high-ranking Minnesota House Democrat and her husband and the attempted murders of a state senator and his wife.

The agreement was part of a plea deal reached ahead of a change-of-plea hearing scheduled […]. New York Post: Federal prosecutors not seeking death penalty in plea deal with man accused of killing top Minnesota Dem.

New York Post reports that the defendant, Vance Boelter, was scheduled for a change-of-plea hearing Thursday morning in federal court in Minneapolis.

links.headline: Federal prosecutors not seeking death penalty in plea deal with man accused of killing top Minnesota Dem
links.excerpt: The defendant, Vance Boelter, was scheduled for a change-of-plea hearing Thursday morning in federal court in Minneapolis.

stories.title: India summons US envoy over attack on ship carrying Indian sailors off Oman
stories.summary:
India summons US envoy over attack on ship carrying Indian sailors off Oman. Three Indian seafarers are missing after Oman rescues 21 others following a US attack on the Palau-flagged Settebello.

BBC: Three Indian sailors missing after US says it hit tanker in Gulf of Oman.

BBC reports that india's government said three Indian sailors were missing and 21 crew had been rescued after the hit on the Settebello off the coast of Oman.

stories.title: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill 17, reports say
stories.summary:
Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon have resulted in at least 17 fatalities, according to recent reports. Nine of those killed were in the town of Tayr Debba, as reported by Lebanon's state news agency.

Separately, Al Jazeera confirmed that an Israeli strike on a vehicle in Sidon killed two people and ignited a fire. The incident occurred in the southern Lebanese city, adding to the rising death toll from the ongoing hostilities.

links.headline: Israeli strike on a car kills two in Sidon, Lebanon
links.excerpt: An Israeli strike on a vehicle in the southern Lebanese city of Sidon killed two people and sparked a fire.
links.imageAlt: Israeli strike on a car kills two in Sidon, Lebanon

stories.title: Consumer watchdog warns real estate agents must not misrepresent homes
stories.summary:
South Australia's consumer watchdog has issued a clear directive to real estate agents, emphasizing that they must not misrepresent information regarding builders or serious defects when selling homes. This warning comes in the wake of eight local builders receiving public warning notices for their conduct.

The regulatory body highlighted the responsibility of agents to ensure accuracy in property disclosures, particularly concerning construction quality and builder reputation. The move aims to protect consumers from potential misinformation that could lead to significant financial or structural issues.

links.headline: Consumer watchdog warns real estate agents must not misrepresent homes
links.excerpt: Real estate agents selling homes must not misrepresent information about the builder or serious defects, SA's consumer watchdog has said, after eight builders were hit with public warning notices over their conduct in the state.
links.imageAlt: Consumer watchdog warns real estate agents must not misrepresent homes

stories.summary:

Australian activists detained by Israel while aboard a flotilla attempting to deliver aid to Gaza return to Sydney, with organisers alleging abuse, sexual assault and beatings, some of which reportedly left detainees hospitalised.

stories.summary:
Why Andrew Yang is building instead of waiting for Washington. Andrew Yang’s 2020 presidential campaign was based on a warning that automation and AI would hollow out the labor market and concentrate wealth in the hands of a few.

At the time, ideas like Universal Basic Income felt fringe.

Now Dario Amodei, Sam Altman, and Bernie Sanders are all saying versions of the same thing. An entrepreneur at heart, […].

stories.title: Luistro: Pre-trial to give peek at how Sara Duterte tackles impeachment
stories.summary:
Luistro: Pre-trial to give peek at how Sara Duterte tackles impeachment. MANILA, Philippines — Both the pre-trial brief and the pre-trial conference for Vice President Sara Duterte’s impeachment trial are of key significance because they will give the prosecution and the public a peek at how her camp will counter the allegations raised against her, Batangas Rep. Gerville Luistro said.

Luistro, in an online interview with reporters. Rappler: VP Sara’s impeachment pre-trial: How it compares to Estrada, Corona.

Rappler reports that a pre-trial conference can help expedite proceedings against Vice President Sara Duterte, and avoid setbacks endured during the Corona trial, which didn't have a preliminary hearing.

stories.summary:
Cut off by quake: Sarangani’s remote towns plead for water, food.

SARANGANI, Philippines — A quiet but worsening humanitarian crisis has unfolded across Sarangani, where isolated communities in Malapatan and Glan continue to grapple with dwindling water supplies, damaged infrastructure, and limited access to relief following the recent magnitude 7.8 earthquake.

Road damage, landslides, and the collapse of critical infrastructure have complicated relief operations and left.

stories.title: ‘Transparency is not optional’: Hacktivists claim Senate website breach
stories.summary:
‘Transparency is not optional’: Hacktivists claim Senate website breach. MANILA, Philippines — “Transparency is not optional.” A Filipino hacktivist group identifying as “Nullsec Philippines” said this after claiming responsibility for the purported defacing of the Senate website.

“The Filipino people entrusted you with power, responsibility, and the duty to serve the nation—not personal interests, political dynasties, or corrupt networks,” Nullsec stressed in a social. Philippine Daily Inquirer: Senate website ‘under maintenance’ after alleged hacking.

stories.title: Navotas fishers renew call to stop Manila Bay reclamation
stories.summary:
Fisherfolk in Navotas renewed their opposition to reclamation projects in Manila Bay on World Oceans Day, June 8. The protest was organized by the Navotas chapter of Pambansang Lakas ng Kilusang Mamamalakaya ng Pilipinas.

The group argued that ongoing developments continue to threaten local livelihoods, coastal communities, and marine ecosystems. Their concerns persist despite a government suspension on reclamation activities in the area.

stories.title: Fair long weekend weather expected as ‘habagat’ to further weaken
stories.summary:
Fair long weekend weather expected as ‘habagat’ to further weaken.

MANILA, Philippines — The southwest monsoon or “habagat” is expected to weaken even further over the long weekend for Independence Day, June 12, indicating possible fairer weather, the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (Pagasa) said.

Habagat refers to warm, moist winds originating from the southwest, which bring rains to the western portion of.

stories.title: They expect you to die! The history of James Bond video games, from the good to the bad to the downright ugly
stories.summary:

stories.title: The Guardian view on NHS records: patients are not raw material for big tech | Editorial
stories.summary:
The Guardian view on NHS records: patients are not raw material for big tech | Editorial.

Ministers should end Palantir’s contract before medical confidentiality is sacrificed to Silicon Valley’s appetite for public data Alarm bells ought to have rung when it emerged last month that Palantir engineers could gain “ unlimited access ” to identifiable NHS patient data.

stories.title: Experts say we should use passkeys, but can a smartphone PIN really be safer than a password?
stories.summary:
The Guardian's long-running reader Q&A series recently tackled a pressing question regarding personal cybersecurity and the adoption of passkeys. A contributor expressed confusion over how authentication methods like smartphone PINs or facial recognition could be considered safer than traditional passwords.

This inquiry highlights the ongoing debate surrounding modern digital security protocols. While experts advocate for the widespread use of passkeys to reduce vulnerability to phishing and data breaches, many users remain skeptical about their practical implementation.

links.headline: Experts say we should use passkeys, but can a smartphone PIN really be safer than a password?
links.excerpt: The long-running series in which readers answer other readers’ questions explores a topical issue of personal cybersecurity Readers reply: If an alien asked you: ‘What is music?’ what would you play for them? I’ve been struggling to get my head around the idea that a passkey, which can be a PIN on your phone, or facial recognition, can be safer than
